text: SitRep50 re-reports the aggregate isolation census at 628 patients (89.4% occupancy) in the banner, unchanged from SitRep49's end-of-day census, but published NO fresh patient-movement table (Tableau 6) this cycle. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is therefore carried from SitRep49's last published split, which still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628); it is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at 136% occupancy (challenge table cites 138.8% saturation).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Continuity of care reports 109 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative in Ituri, of whom 34 died (31.2%) and 46 recovered. 774 hot meals were served (150 to confirmed cases, 127 to suspected cases, 446 to PPLs, 52 to caregivers).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: 26 new confirmed cases including 9 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-03, in Ituri (21 including 6 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (5 including 3 deaths). In total 19 confirmed deaths were recorded over the day: the 9 community deaths plus 10 deaths among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (8 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing confirmed deaths to 492.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: The 26 new cases came from Rwampara (11), Bunia (5), Mongbwalu (3) and Mangala (2) in Ituri and from Butembo (2), Musienene (1), Masereka (1) and Beni (1) in Nord-Kivu.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-03; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

text: Nord-Kivu cumulative counts were re-actualized this cycle: alongside 5 new confirmations the province table shows a within-province reclassification (Goma 2->1, Kyondo 3->4), a net +5 consistent with DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization.

text: 10 patients recovered (9 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 239. Mongbwalu carries a 50.4% case-fatality (139 deaths for 276 cases); Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 58.0%, led by Katwa (72.5%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%).

text: Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 93 samples with 5 positive (5.4%). Sud-Kivu collected 5 samples still under analysis. New diagnostic labs are being installed at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa and Nia-Nia (Ituri).

text: A dead body arriving from Uganda toward the DRC (Beni) was intercepted at the Kasindi PoE; 3 dead bodies were intercepted at PoE/PoC overall. A security incident (vandalism) was reported at PoC Irumu Centre.

text: Community-death analysis (SitRep50): the majority of recorded deaths are community deaths, concentrated in Mongbwalu, Bunia and Katwa, with late presentation to health structures raising the risk of secondary household transmission. These sub-cohort figures are qualitative context and are not used as denominators for the 492 confirmed-death total.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: reticence_post_mortem - Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities. - Response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: case_load_and_capacity - Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk. - Response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: contact_followup_gap - Contact follow-up performance reached 81.5% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted. - Response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: weak_alert_reporting - Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission. - Response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: unlocalized_confirmed_cases - Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction. - Response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_medicines_gap - Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management. - Response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ipc_and_hcw_infections - Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%). - Response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: lab_backlog - A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table). - Response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ambulance_isolation_gap - Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk. - Response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: coordination_gap - Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions. - Response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: financial_resource_gap - A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars. - Response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: insecurity_access - Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones. - Response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_services_continuity - Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality. - Response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: population_mobility - High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; this cycle a dead body arriving from Uganda toward the DRC was intercepted at the Kasindi PoE (Beni, Nord-Kivu), and the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases stay counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri). - Response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Source review / Before zone-level attribution use: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Case management / Urgent: Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 58.0% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction (new CTE site identified in Bunia; Nizi and Nyankunde sites evaluated)
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.
